description: Flavour hooks for how the PC acquired their first ship. Longer prose lives in docs/player_ship_origin_stories.md.

origins.title: Won in a gamble
origins.summary: A merchant could not cover his losses; the hull passed to you before witnesses. The harbour argues whether the throw was fair.

origins.title: Inheritance from a relative
origins.summary: A distant kinsman died and named you heir to a small vessel and a modest purse after priests and tax-farmers took their cut.

origins.title: Bought from a failed house
origins.summary: Magistrates sold a bankrupt merchant's assets; you were quickest with silver and a clean enough name.

origins.title: Taken in settlement of a debt
origins.summary: A debtor offered the hull when coin and grain would not cover what was owed; partners may call it theft with a seal.

origins.title: Old captain's bequest
origins.summary: Your late master left you the ship if you buried him ashore and paid his debts; you did both.

origins.title: Salvage (or a thin story of it)
origins.summary: A hull found awash, claimed and refitted; tavern talk disagrees on what became of her crew.

origins.title: Surplus levy hull
origins.summary: After a levy stood down, the arsenal sold slow auxiliaries; you bought one at auction with pooled savings and a guarantor.

origins.title: Charter that became title
origins.summary: The owner sickened and heirs quarrelled; you kept contracts and crew fed until documents made you master in fact.

origins.title: Partner vanished, hull left
origins.summary: A partner who knew coves did not return; you paid slip arrears, renamed her, and sail as if you had always owned her.

origins.title: Staked on a race
origins.summary: A headland course with the patched merchantman as stake; you won by a length in a gust your rival's friends call bought.

origins.title: Forfeited collateral
origins.summary: You held paper against the ship; the borrower fled; the magistrate ruled forfeit after due notice.
